questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Which of the following reaction is feasible?
questions.solution:
Reaction in (a) is not feasible because replacement of $-\mathrm{OH}$ by $-\mathrm{Cl}$ is not possible for phenol due to partial $\mathrm{C}$ $=\mathrm{O}$ bond and resonance stabilization.
Reaction is (c) is not feasible because $\mathrm{KMnO}_4$ is a strong oxidizing agent and converts $-\mathrm{C}-\mathrm{OH}$ to $-\mathrm{COOH}$.
Reaction in (d) is not feasible because $20 \% \mathrm{PO}_4$ is not a strong enough dehydrating agent and therefore $85 \% \mathrm{~h}_3$ $\mathrm{PO}_4$ is used.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A,B,C,D in the following reactions are
questions.solution:
Salicylic acid will give phenol with CaO and NaOH. When carboxylic acids are heated with sodalime, they undergo decarboxylation. 

When phenol is distilled with Zn-dust, it gives benzene.

The reaction on salicylic acid with bromine water is an example of oxidation and electrophilic substitution reaction. Salycylic acid in this reaction converts to 2,4,6-tribromo phenol.

When salicylic acid is heated, it decomposes to phenol and carbon dioxide.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
Statement (A) Carboxylic acid can be obtained by the reaction of $R-\mathrm{C} \equiv \mathrm{N}$ with $\mathrm{H}_3 \stackrel{\oplus}{\mathrm{O}}$ under mild reaction conditions.
Statement (B) Hydrolysis of $R-\mathrm{C} \equiv \mathrm{N}$ in alkaline aqueous medium gives $R-\mathrm{COON} \mathrm{Na}$ and $\mathrm{NH}_3$ as products.
The correct answer is
options: (A) is correct, but (B) is not correct
(A) is not correct but (B) is correct
questions.solution:
Carboxylic acid can be obtained by complete hydrolysis of $R-\mathrm{CN}$. Therefore, statement $(A)$ is incorrect.
When alkyl cyanide hydrolysed in presence of alkaline medium, $R-\mathrm{COOO}^{-}+\mathrm{a}-\mathrm{Na}$ and ammonia gas are obtained
Therefore, statement (4) is correct.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

$$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The total number of $\mathrm{S}_{\mathrm{N}} 2$ (nucleophilic substitution bimolecular) reactions involved in the following reaction sequence is
questions.solution: Total number of $\mathrm{S}_{\mathrm{N}} 2$ reactions involved in the reaction is three. In first $\mathrm{S}_{\mathrm{N}} 2$ reaction methoxy group is replaced by $\mathrm{OH}$ group (i.e. $\mathrm{CH}_3$ group replace by $\mathrm{H}^{+}$of $\mathrm{HI}$ ). In second $\mathrm{S}_{\mathrm{N}} 2$ reaction another methoxy group get replaced by $\mathrm{OH}$. In third $\mathrm{S}_{\mathrm{N}} 2$ reaction $\mathrm{OH}$ get replaced by iodide group.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Compound ' \(A\) ' undergoes formation of cyanohydrins, which on hydrolysis gives lactic acid \(\left(\mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{CHOHCOOH}\right)\). Therefore, compound ' \(A\) ' is ____
questions.solution:
Acetaldehyde on reaction with hydrogen cyanide gives cyanohydrin which on hydrolysis gives lactic acid.
Therefore compound \(A\) is acetaldehyde. Hence, the correct option is (b).
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q:
Which of the following acids does not contain
-COOH group?
questions.solution:
Carbamic acid $\quad \mathrm{HOOC}-\mathrm{NH}_{2}$ (b) Barbituric acid $\quad \mathrm{C}_{4} \mathrm{H}_{4} \mathrm{~N}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{3}$ (c) Lactic acid
(d) Succinic acid HOOC $-\mathrm{CH}_{2}-\mathrm{CH}_{2}-\mathrm{COOH}$ Therefore, barbituric acid is actually not an acid.

questions.q: The major product form by the monobromination of phenyl benzoate is
questions.solution: In phenyl benzoate  the 1string gets deactivated, while 2nd ring gets activated due to lone pairs on oxygen atom. So it can give ortho or para bromo product. But the ortho position is sterically hindered, so it will give para compound as a major product.

questions.q: Surbhi need to convert acetic acid to ethanol for experimental purpose, which of the following reagents should be employed?
questions.solution:
LiAlH4 + Ether is a reducing agent.
It reduces all types of carboxylic acids. So this reagent can be used to convert acetic acid into ethanol.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Which is more stable?
questions.solution:
In X structure,OH2+ group is formed which is a good leaving group. Hence, X is highly unstable.

In Y structure,resonance is observed and before protonation, 2 resonanting structures are possible which are given below:

In carboxylic acid the oxygen attached to carboxyl carbon is more electronegative and withdraws the electrons of bond. Hence, proton gets attached to the negatively charged carbon.

This is why Y structure is more stable than X.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: According to IUPAC convention, name of the following compound is
options: Both B and C are correct
questions.solution:
The IUPAC name of this compound is ethyl-3-chloro-5-ethylbenzoate.
Ester group has the highest priority hence ethyl benzoate. Chloro and ethyl are numbered alphabetically because they are equidistant.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: Which of the following is a tricarboxylic acid?
questions.solution:
A tricarboxylic acid is an  organic carboxylic acid  whose  chemical structure contains three  carboxyl functional groups  (−COOH). The best-known example of a tricarboxylic acid is  citric acid.

Citric Acid

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
The correct increasing order of acidities is:
Cl3CCOOH, Cl2CHCOOH, ClCH2COOH
questions.solution:
Greater the number of electron-withdrawing substituents for a conjugate base, more is the stability of conjugate base. More the stability of conjugate base, more is the acidity. Here, chlorine is an electron-withdrawing group. Thus, the order of acidity is,
1<2<3
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: An optically active compound <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi></mfenced></math> has molecular formula <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mn>4</mn></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mn>8</mn></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math>. It evolves <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>CO</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> with <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>NaHCO</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math>. <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi></mfenced></math> reacts with <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>LiAlH</mi><mn>4</mn></msub></math> to give an achiral compound. Structure of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi></mfenced></math> is.
questions.solution:
An optically active compound is the one which has a chiral centre. A chiral centre is the one with all different substituent attached.

Carboxylic acid on reaction with NaHCO3 gives CO2 and on reaction with LiAlH4, it reduces to alcoholic group.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
